codeReview.summary: The transcript, handoff, and commit diff all align with the requested Spotless/gate-fast cleanup. Although the commit touches 122 Java source/test files, a whitespace-normalized review showed the changes are formatter/import-order rewrites plus a couple of long string literal wraps in `DefaultAuditAccessService` and `ValidationSeedDataInitializer`; I did not find any executable behavior change or contract regression in production code. The worker also captured successful `spotless:check`, repo `gate_fast.sh`, and explicit `mvn test -Pgate-fast -Djacoco.skip=true` evidence for the committed state, so the feature meets the requested formatting baseline without introducing a high-confidence defect.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `backend-worker` skill does not explicitly call out formatter-only packets in Step 2, so workers still have to infer that Spotless plus existing regression suites is the correct proof shape for this kind of feature.
